mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.02 during periods of declining FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.4pp, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative average 60-day forward return or a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a high-inflation environment signals supply-side bottlenecks that force monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO declines during periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% while inflation remains above 3%, aggressive monetary policy compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The threshold condition triggers positive 20-day returns in over 60% of historical occurrences, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.02, or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of positive payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while retail sales accelerate indicates unsustainable consumer borrowing that precedes a demand collapse and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling inflation signals improving supply chain efficiency and margin expansion, which generates immediate positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL declines, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 2-year rolling windows.
